kept.text:
Grade:  A-.

Nailed the pharma callouts for their earnings week.  Pizza, car companies, all dumped.  $CAT and $BE didn't disappoint on numbers - but did move more than I anticipated. Taking cash off the table of the hard drive makers was smart, and the megacaps - $GOOGL notwithstanding and the only real blemish on this analysis - did exactly what was expected.

profile_bio.description: Retired VC; PhD economics (Sloan); sardonic humorist. SAR. USCG. Pilot. I have more hit points than you can possibly imagine. If you @grok, you get the block.

quoted_tweet.text:
Market analysis for the week:  Part of this could actually be labeled a market analysis for the summer.

Here's the good news:  The markets have finally started to ignore Trump's on-again-off-again war crimes bonanza, so it's slightly less headline driven than it has been the last couple of months.  And the theatrical rendition of the correspondent's dinner over the weekend isn't going to move it.

Here's the not-so-good news:  On the shorter time-frame, this is the most important week of the quarter from an earnings perspective, with 5 of the 7 most important tickers on the market reporting.  And, unfortunately for those looking for easy wins, 2 of them ( $META, $MSFT) are at levels that have resulted in them being punished regardless of earnings previously (420 and 700, respectively).  2 more - $AMZN and $GOOGL - are trading at all time highs.  This puts all 4 of their upside potentials - and, by proxy due to their weight in the indices, the entire market - in a very shaky place.

When we look at the rest of the earnings calendar, there are even more vertical inducing minefields.  $DPZ reports before the bell, and while you'll be forgiven thinking they're unimportant, they speak to the health of the consumer and the odds are really good they're going to say the consumer has problems.  $GM takes a swing on Tuesday; $F and $CMG on Wednesday...same story.  The credit card companies are on deck this week - they'll probably be OK, since $AXP led the way last week.

Then we have the AI infrastructure trade.  Cavebaby classic $BE and yes-it's-an-AI-stock $CAT are up - both are going to report strong numbers, but they're at such eye-watering valuations right now that I'm not sure it will matter.  Top-5-stocks-of-the-year darlings $STX, $WDC and $SNDK are also in this week.  You're up an absolutely insane amount on these tickers, and they've got plenty more to give you....but maybe pull back enough cash to buy that new fishing boat with some of your gains, just to be sure due to the headwinds above (and now, below)

That other headwind?  Longtime followers have heard me talk about this for years now, but the old "sell in May and go away" phenomena.  While an outdated practice in the era of digital access to portfolios (this philosophy dates back to 18th century England and was a common practice in the US dating to about the 70's as institutional investors went to summer at Cape Cod) , it still has legs in the modern market.  For those who don't know, it's the concept of old money selling assets in May for the summer months because A) they'll be traveling and B) because returns during the summer months tend to be muted, so the risk:reward ratio gets out of skew.  With markets at all time highs, geopolitical uncertainty, the fact we can't go a single damn weekend without Trump trying to ruin it for everybody, and pent up luxury travel demand this year, there are a LOT more headwinds than tailwinds this week when you combine everything in the prior paragraphs with the fact that there WILL be selling to close out this week as liquidity leaves the markets.

There's also a note I want to make about China's industrial output and what it means for my EOY recommendation last year to look to emerging markets for the best opportunities (which was correct), but I've got a whole other post on that in the hopper for later in the day.  Just understand that there was data released overnight that will further make people aware that these markets are a safer haven than our own.

TL:DR:  Danger!  Danger Will Robinson!

(And, if you made it this far, pharma stocks look interesting this week - $LLY, $MRK, and $MRNA.  But understand the structure before just blindly jumping in to think you know what I mean by that)

$CAT being retardio gunned on those shitty recips is crazy. This is not a LT solution, it is just the shortest cycle option to get BTM power. They are too small, dirty, loud, and unrealiable to power large DCs LT

The entire point is you could actually get them, now that backlog is blown out too. Batteries and SOFCs are infinitely better for the one advantages they have which is spinning up fast

PORTFOLIO UPDATE 5/1
$IBKR   $LUNR   $QBTS   $ORCL   $TSLA   $XYZ   $MU   $CAVA   $ARM   $BE   $IBIT   $GEV   $VRT   $VRT   $CAT   $NBIS   $NBIS

Yoooo! 

Lockout rally remains, with another new high today, as breadth & momentum starting to pick up again after the last 2 weeks digestion (yes, yes, if you only look at QQQ you don't know, but you have to look under the hood). New exposure doing well, and I was able to de-risk 2 today. My goal remains to do less, let that exposure work, and let that trend unfold while reducing exposure into strength in the next few days & weeks. 

NER dropped at -1.15% as I trimmed NBIS/TSLA, and closed CAVA add. Open Heat at -12%, with a growing Open Delta of +20.6% on this market cycle. 

Cheers HAGW!
